title: SACRAMENTO, CA
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: It is more cost-effective to keep people in their existing homes than to intervene after they become homeless.
Roughly 64 percent of low-income households in California are rent-burdened and unable to afford basic needs.
Providing short and medium term rent subsidies of up to $2,000 a month could help the most housing insecure populations remain in their homes.
A two-year rent subsidy program funded at $500 million could prevent homelessness for more than 10,000 people.
The cost of supportive housing can reach roughly $600,000 per new unit.
Young people aging out of foster care are often not prepared to survive independently, increasing their risk of homelessness.
The age for foster youth to receive support through the Independent Living Program is proposed to be increased from age 21 to 23.
Regional planning programs for housing development often rely on inconsistent data and presumptions.
Incentivizing sustainable housing near transit can help reduce reliance on cars.
